title: Reference-picker server-side search + pagination for large option sets
business_reason: The lookup endpoints returned up to 1000 rows and the picker rendered them all — slow and unusable for large tables (candidates, recipients). Add server-side label search (?q=) and a small page cap (?limit=, default 50, hasMore flag) so big option sets stay fast.

design.shared_helper: server/lookups/query.ts — lookupQuery(?q=,?limit= default 50/max 200), escapeLike (so a typed % / _ is literal, not a wildcard), lookupResult (fetch limit+1 -> {items, hasMore}). Used by BOTH staff + school endpoints so behaviour is identical.
design.endpoints: both /api/(staff|school)/lookup apply ilike(labelCol, q) AFTER the scoping filter (search never widens scope) and cap at limit+1; school endpoint applies it on the final target query of every strategy (direct/path/junction).
design.component: FieldInput reference picker debounces typing (250ms) and refetches ?q=&limit=50; a search box appears only when the set is large (hasMore) or a search is active, with a 'showing the first 50 — type to narrow' hint. The search input has an aria-label (a11y gate).

param_14.question: Do large reference pickers stay usable?
param_14.answer: Yes. Reference pickers now search server-side: typing in the picker (debounced) calls the lookup with ?q= and shows the first 50 matches with a 'type to narrow' hint when there are more. The endpoints cap results (limit+1 to detect hasMore) and apply the label search AFTER scoping, so a school's search never widens beyond its own rows. Verified: a 15-row table with limit=2 returns 2 + hasMore=true, and a no-match query returns 0.
param_14.security_implications: search is applied after the school-scoping filter (never widens scope); ILIKE wildcards in user input are escaped; results capped (<=200) so no unbounded payloads.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: keyset/offset pagination if a single filtered set still exceeds the cap; recently-used ordering.

verify: live: limit=2 on 15-row table -> 2 items + hasMore=true; q=nomatch -> 0
